title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 2 (Book 3)
source: CPE Practice Tests 3, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 1: Cloze Texts

The Kingfisher

It was still dark as she (1) ______ down the back stairs and drew back the bolt of the kitchen door as noiselessly as possible. It made a loud (2) ______ sound. She must remember to oil it! Every time it happened she thought that, but never remembered until the next time it made the same noise. It never seemed to wake the (3) ______ of the house, though. Probably because it was so early. The grass was damp with dew as she crossed the lawn in front of the house. Wet feet, again. She should have worn her wellingtons, but she didn’t know where they were. Sheila could have borrowed them. She often borrowed them, even though they were about three (4) ______ too big. Sheila was careless and was always losing things. She heard bird (5) ______ as she reached the river bank and saw the first (6) ______ of the sun shining over the Doonagh Hills.

Bedtime Reading

Can you see yourself logging off, turning off and unplugging, before you turn off your light? Many people are saying now that this is precisely what we will all be doing in a few years’ time. They are prophesying the demise of the book. Leaving your device on your bedside (7) ______ is a routine for many already. A new (8) ______ of life for the rainforests, they say, another coup for technology.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'crept' (quá khứ của creep) nghĩa là đi rón rén, bò nhẹ nhàng để không gây tiếng động. Phù hợp tuyệt đối với cụm 'as noiselessly as possible' (không tiếng động nhất có thể). Bẫy: 'slithered' là trườn (như rắn), 'crawled' là bò sát đất bằng đầu gối/tay (như em bé), 'bounded' là nhảy chồm lên hướng về phía trước. Chuyên đề: Động từ di chuyển. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: crept down the back stairs and drew back the bolt ... noiselessly
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'squeaking sound' = tiếng rít, tiếng cọt kẹt của cửa gỗ hay kim loại thiếu dầu. Phù hợp với vế sau 'She must remember to oil it!' (phải nhớ tra dầu cho nó). Bẫy: 'screeching' là tiếng rít phanh xe hoặc tiếng kêu la chói tai, 'screaming' là tiếng thét sợ hãi của con người, 'squealing' là tiếng kêu thé lên của lợn hoặc âm thanh kéo dài bern bánh xe. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Âm thanh vật lý.
questions.answerLocation: It made a loud squeaking sound. She must remember to oil it!
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'the rest of the house' là cụm từ cố định nghĩa là 'phần còn lại của ngôi nhà' hoặc ở đây là 'những người khác đang ngủ trong nhà'. Bẫy: 'remains' là hài cốt/di tích, 'last' là người cuối cùng, 'others' là những cái khác/người khác (thường không đi sau 'the' trong cấu trúc này mà phải là 'the others' đứng độc lập làm chủ ngữ). Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: never seemed to wake the rest of the house, though.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'three sizes too big' = rộng hơn 3 cỡ. Đây là cấu trúc đo lường độ chênh lệch kích cỡ trang phục, giày dép chuẩn trong tiếng Anh. Bẫy: 'figures' là số liệu/hình dáng, 'pairs' là cặp/đôi, 'numbers' là con số — các từ này không dùng để diễn đạt kích thước chênh lệch cỡ giày. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Kích thước trang phục.
questions.answerLocation: even though they were about three sizes too big.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'bird song' (hoặc 'birdsong') là danh từ ghép cố định chỉ tiếng chim hót líu lo tự nhiên buổi sáng. Bẫy: 'tune' là giai điệu bài hát của con người, 'cry' là tiếng kêu khóc/kêu cứu của động vật, 'music' là âm nhạc nghệ thuật. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: She heard bird song as she reached the river bank
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'rays of the sun' (tia nắng mặt trời) là cụm từ cố định chỉ các luồng ánh sáng mặt trời chiếu xuống mặt đất khi bình minh lên. Bẫy: 'sparkles' là các hạt lấp lánh, 'lights' là nguồn sáng chung chung, 'gleams' là những tia sáng yếu ớt/lập lòe. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Ánh sáng tự nhiên.
questions.answerLocation: saw the first rays of the sun shining over the Doonagh Hills.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'bedside table' là cụm từ chỉ chiếc bàn nhỏ cạnh giường ngủ chuyên dùng để đèn ngủ, sách, hoặc điện thoại. Bẫy: 'sideboard' là tủ bát đĩa phòng ăn, 'wardrobe' là tủ quần áo lớn, 'commode' là tủ kéo nhỏ kiểu Pháp cổ hoặc bệ vệ sinh di động. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng đồ nội thất.
questions.answerLocation: Leaving your device on your bedside table is a routine
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'a new lease of life' là idiom cố định có nghĩa là 'một nguồn sinh lực mới, một cuộc đời mới'. Ở đây ám chỉ e-book giúp giảm thiểu khai thác rừng làm giấy, mang lại sinh lực mới cho các cánh rừng. Bẫy: 'treaty' là hiệp ước quốc tế, 'contract' là hợp đồng pháp lý, 'mortgage' là khoản vay thế chấp ngân hàng. Chuyên đề: Idioms — a new lease of life.
questions.answerLocation: A new lease of life for the rainforests, they say
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
A University Education

A few generations ago, a university education could only be aspired TO by an elite minority. In the (9) ______ few decades, however, a change has slowly but (10) ______ been taking place and prospective students who, a century ago, would have known that (11) ______ a basic education was beyond (12) ______ reach, can now look forward to gaining a degree in higher education.

Universities have been transformed (13) ______ places not only of learning, but also of business and are churning out on a regular (14) ______ their finished product — the graduate. However, degree-holders are still faced (15) ______ a problem. (16) ______ used to be a passport to employment is no longer a guarantee of success.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (9): In the ______ few decades, however...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: PAST (hoặc LAST). Cụm từ 'in the past/last few decades' nghĩa là 'trong một vài thập kỷ qua'. Sử dụng tính từ 'past' hoặc 'last' để biểu thị khoảng thời gian kéo dài từ quá khứ gần đến hiện tại. Chuyên đề: Determiners & Time expressions.
questions.answerLocation: In the past few decades, however, a change has slowly but surely...
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (10): a change has slowly but ______ been taking place
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SURELY. Thành ngữ cố định 'slowly but surely' mang nghĩa là 'chậm mà chắc / tuy chậm nhưng chắc chắn'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: a change has slowly but surely been taking place
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (11): would have known that ______ a basic education was beyond...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EVEN (hoặc các danh động từ như GETTING / OBTAINING / HAVING / ACQUIRING). Trạng từ nhấn mạnh 'even' tạo ra ý nghĩa cực cấp: 'ngay cả một nền giáo dục cơ bản cũng vượt tầm tay của họ'. Ngoài ra, một danh động từ chỉ việc có được giáo dục ('getting/having') cũng hoàn toàn phù hợp về mặt ngữ pháp và ngữ nghĩa. Chuyên đề: Adverbs / Gerunds.
questions.answerLocation: would have known that even a basic education was beyond...
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (12): education was beyond ______ reach
questions.explanation: Đáp án: THEIR. Tính từ sở hữu 'their' bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'reach' (tầm tay) nhằm ám chỉ 'tầm tay của những học sinh đầy triển vọng này'. 'Beyond their reach' = vượt ngoài khả năng/tầm với của họ. Chuyên đề: Pronouns. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: would have known that even a basic education was beyond their reach
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (13): Universities have been transformed ______ places...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: INTO. Cấu trúc bị động 'be transformed into something' nghĩa là 'được biến đổi/chuyển hóa thành cái gì'. Giới từ 'into' chỉ sự chuyển giao trạng thái hoàn toàn. Chuyên đề: Prepositions.
questions.answerLocation: Universities have been transformed into places not only of learning
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (14): churning out on a regular ______ their finished product
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BASIS. Cụm từ cố định chỉ tần suất 'on a regular basis' đồng nghĩa với trạng từ 'regularly' (một cách thường xuyên, đều đặn). Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: are churning out on a regular basis their finished product
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (15): degree-holders are still faced ______ a problem
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITH. Cấu trúc bị động cố định 'be faced with something' nghĩa là 'đối mặt/đối đầu với một vấn đề, khó khăn'. Chuyên đề: Prepositions.
questions.answerLocation: degree-holders are still faced with a problem.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (16): ______ used to be a passport to employment is...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHAT (phải viết hoa chữ W đầu câu). Đại từ quan hệ 'What' (Cái mà) đứng đầu câu dẫn dắt một mệnh đề danh ngữ đóng vai trò làm chủ ngữ chính cho toàn bộ câu: 'What used to be a passport to employment is...' Chuyên đề: Relative clauses & Noun clauses.
questions.answerLocation: What used to be a passport to employment is no longer...
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Laughter

Is there anyone who doesn’t enjoy a good laugh? I think not. Well, you may still be surprised to hear that researchers have recently estimated that laughing 100 times is the equivalent to a 15-minute (17) ______ (WORK). They explain that when we laugh, not only does our blood (18) ______ (PRESS) drop, but the flow of the blood improves, which in turn aids (19) ______ (HEAL). Laughter also exercises other parts of your body, including your diaphragm, leg, back, and of course (20) ______ (FACE) muscles.

This all sounds very nice, but is there any proof? Well, according to doctors dealing with patients on a regular basis, the (21) ______ (PSYCHOLOGY) advantages to be gained from laughter are (22) ______ (COMPARE). Those suffering from depression were found to make vast improvements when introduced to laughter-generating situations. Apparently the message is crystal clear: if you feel stressed or miserable there is nothing as (23) ______ (THERAPY) as laughing. Although it has so far been hard to (24) ______ (VALUE) the results on a long-term basis, it is hoped that further research will prove its lasting benefits.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct transformed word in each gap.

questions.instruction: 17. WORK ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WORKOUT. Từ gốc 'WORK' (làm việc) được biến đổi thành danh từ ghép 'workout' nghĩa là 'buổi tập thể dục, bài rèn luyện thể chất'. Tiếng cười tương đương với một buổi tập thể dục ngắn 15 phút. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Compound nouns.
questions.answerLocation: equivalent to a 15-minute workout
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 18. PRESS ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: PRESSURE. Động từ 'PRESS' (nhấn, ép) chuyển đổi thành danh từ chỉ sự đè nén/áp lực 'pressure'. Ghép với từ 'blood' đứng trước tạo thành cụm danh từ 'blood pressure' (huyết áp). Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: not only does our blood pressure drop
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 19. HEAL ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: HEALING. Động từ 'HEAL' (chữa lành) được biến đổi thành danh động từ/danh từ 'healing' (quá trình phục hồi/chữa lành bệnh tật) đứng sau động từ 'aids' (hỗ trợ). Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: which in turn aids healing
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 20. FACE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FACIAL. Danh từ 'FACE' (khuôn mặt) được biến đổi thành tính từ 'facial' (thuộc về khuôn mặt) bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'muscles' đứng sau. Cụm 'facial muscles' = cơ mặt. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: and of course facial muscles
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 21. PSYCHOLOGY ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: PSYCHOLOGICAL. Danh từ 'PSYCHOLOGY' (tâm lý học) được chuyển hóa thành tính từ 'psychological' (thuộc về tâm lý) để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'advantages' đứng sau. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: the psychological advantages to be gained from laughter
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 22. COMPARE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: INCOMPARABLE. Động từ 'COMPARE' (so sánh) được biến đổi thành tính từ mang tiền tố phủ định cực cấp 'incomparable' (không thể so sánh được/vô song/tuyệt vời hơn mọi thứ khác) để đóng vai trò làm bổ ngữ cho danh từ số nhiều đứng sau động từ 'are'. Tiếng cười mang lại những lợi ích vô song. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Negative prefixes.
questions.answerLocation: advantages to be gained from laughter are incomparable.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 23. THERAPY ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: THERAPEUTIC. Danh từ 'THERAPY' (liệu pháp điều trị) biến đổi thành tính từ 'therapeutic' (mang tính trị liệu, có tác dụng điều trị bệnh) đứng sau cấu trúc so sánh bằng 'as... as'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: there is nothing as therapeutic as laughing
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 24. VALUE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EVALUATE (hoặc giữ nguyên dạng động từ 'VALUE'). Động từ 'evaluate' nghĩa là 'đánh giá, định lượng' kết quả của tiếng cười trên góc độ khoa học lâu dài. Đứng sau cấu trúc nguyên mẫu chỉ mục đích 'hard to + V-bare'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: hard to evaluate the results on a long term basis
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the second sentence using the key word in bold.

questions.instruction: As I grow older, I distrust other people more and more. (distrustful) -> The older I grow, ____________________ other people.
acceptedAnswers: the more distrustful I become of
the more distrustful I am of
the more distrustful of other people I become
questions.correctAnswer: the more distrustful I become of
questions.explanation: Đáp án: THE MORE DISTRUSTFUL I BECOME OF (hoặc THE MORE DISTRUSTFUL I AM OF). Câu sử dụng cấu trúc so sánh kép 'The + comparative (Adj/Adv), the + comparative (Adj/Adv)'. Kết hợp với tính từ 'distrustful' đi kèm giới từ cố định 'of' (distrustful of somebody = nghi ngờ ai đó). Do đó, vế biến đổi thành 'the more distrustful I become of/I am of'. Chuyên đề: Double comparatives & Adjective prepositions.
questions.answerLocation: the more distrustful I become of
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: You must fasten your seatbelt before take-off. (done) -> Seatbelts ____________________ securely before take-off.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MUST BE DONE UP (hoặc HAVE TO BE DONE UP). Sử dụng phrasal verb 'do up' đồng nghĩa với động từ 'fasten' (thắt chặt, cài khóa). Vì câu gốc ở thể chủ động bắt buộc 'You must fasten...', câu viết lại đổi sang bị động khuyết thiếu: 'Seatbelts must be done up'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs & Passive voice. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: must be done up
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: The competition was so strong that he didn’t stand a chance of winning the race. (strength) -> ____________________ the competition that he didn’t stand a chance of winning the race.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SUCH WAS THE STRENGTH OF. Sử dụng cấu trúc đảo ngữ nhấn mạnh cực độ với 'Such': 'Such + be + Noun + that + Clause' (tương đương với 'So + Adj + be + Noun + that...'). Câu gốc 'The competition was so strong that...' được viết lại thành 'Such was the strength of the competition that...'. Chuyên đề: Inversion with Such/So.
questions.answerLocation: Such was the strength of
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: The minister was heavily criticised for her decision to raise taxes. (subjected) -> The Minister ____________________ criticism for her decision to raise taxes.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WAS SUBJECTED TO HEAVY. Sử dụng cụm từ cố định 'be subjected to something' nghĩa là 'phải gánh chịu/bị đặt vào tình cảnh chịu đựng điều gì tiêu cực'. Trạng từ 'heavily' chuyển thành tính từ 'heavy' đi kèm danh từ 'criticism' (được chuyển đổi từ động từ 'criticised'). Chuyên đề: Collocations & Passive structures.
questions.answerLocation: was subjected to heavy
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I really felt relieved on completion of my thesis. (sense) -> I felt ____________________ when I had completed my thesis.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: A REAL SENSE OF RELIEF. Sử dụng cụm danh từ trừu tượng cố định 'a sense of relief' (cảm giác nhẹ nhõm/giải tỏa). Trạng từ 'really' bổ nghĩa cho động từ 'felt' được chuyển đổi thành tính từ 'real' bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'sense'. Chuyên đề: Collocations & Part of speech change.
questions.answerLocation: a real sense of relief
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I particularly dislike it when I have to cancel appointments. (call) -> What I particularly ____________________ appointments.
acceptedAnswers: dislike is having to call off
dislike is when I have to call off
questions.correctAnswer: dislike is having to call off
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DISLIKE IS HAVING TO CALL OFF. Sử dụng phrasal verb 'call off' đồng nghĩa với động từ 'cancel' (hủy bỏ). Kết hợp cấu trúc câu nhấn mạnh (Cleft sentence) với What: 'What I particularly dislike is + V-ing/Noun Clause'. Từ đó ta có: 'What I particularly dislike is having to call off appointments'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs & Cleft sentences.
questions.answerLocation: dislike is having to call off
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

25. As I grow older, I distrust other people more and more. (distrustful)

The older I grow, ____________________ other people.

26. You must fasten your seatbelt before take-off. (done)

Seatbelts ____________________ securely before take-off.

27. The competition was so strong that he didn’t stand a chance of winning the race. (strength)

____________________ the competition that he didn’t stand a chance of winning the race.

28. The minister was heavily criticised for her decision to raise taxes. (subjected)

The Minister ____________________ criticism for her decision to raise taxes.

29. I really felt relieved on completion of my thesis. (sense)

I felt ____________________ when I had completed my thesis.

30. I particularly dislike it when I have to cancel appointments. (call)

What I particularly ____________________ appointments.

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about phobias. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Hidden Fears

Recently I found myself in the dentist's waiting room, with a large number of people before me. I picked a magazine at random from the table and, a few minutes later, found myself deeply engrossed in an article about phobias. I was fascinated. I’d never thought too much about it. I knew some people were afraid of certain things. I perceived these people as being mostly women. Maybe I had been conditioned from an early age by cartoons showing women jumping up on tables when they saw a mouse, or running out of rooms screaming if they saw a spider. Also, if you had asked me, I would have said that nearly all people’s phobias were about things like rodents or insects.

My eyes were opened by this article which was based on the results of a survey. Thousands of people said they had a strong fear of flying, but most of them were too embarrassed to admit it to their friends. There were many with rodent and insect phobias, phobias about snakes, birds, cats, dogs and horses. Some are afraid of open spaces and some are afraid of enclosed spaces. It seems there is a phobia to suit every taste and pocket, and many of us cope with mild phobias on a daily basis.

When asked if they could find a reason for their phobias, people's answers varied. There were cases of, say, a dog bite in childhood causing a subsequent fear and hatred of dogs, or someone who had been badly cut by a kitchen knife being afraid of blades of all sorts. But it is arguable that these cases are not really phobias, because one of the main hallmarks of a phobia is that it is completely irrational. Strictly speaking, that is the difference between a fear and a phobia. If you are terrified of a poisonous snake or spider that could do you harm or even kill you, that is logical. It makes sense. It is, in a word, rational; a natural human instinct to keep away from danger. However, being terrified by a tiny spider, a small mouse or even a large cockroach makes no sense at all. In an equal battle situation, a human being would win hands down every time. It's simply a matter of size.

So, what is it that makes people scream and squirm when the unfortunate insect runs for cover? People surveyed said that part of the answer, for these types of phobias, anyway, may be found in our senses of sight and touch. A spider that is still - or dead — does not usually inspire too much terror. A spider in full health, on the other hand, walking across the living-room floor, is what does it. It seems to be the movement of the spider's legs that causes the phobic reaction. Other people described a similar reaction to birds’ claws. What is interesting is that nearly everyone imagined the spider or the bird touching them. This is the stuff of nightmares and horror films, and the reason why people who like snakes or keep tarantulas as pets are thought to be weird and twisted. Not one of those questioned could explain why the gentle touch of a spider's legs, say, on their arm would cause them such horror.

A final series of questions in the survey asked people how they managed to live with their phobias and whether they were trying to do something about them. Answers, again, varied. Claustrophobics mostly said that they avoided going into small rooms whenever possible, though they had to do it sometimes, and likewise agoraphobics with wide open spaces. They managed, they said. Others with a phobia about common domestic animals like cats and dogs said that they had tried to tackle it. Having children, for example, who want a pet can be a good way to do this. They nearly all admitted, though, that they hated the animal and would never go near it unless absolutely necessary. Though many people said that they had managed to make some progress, it was a clear case of mind over matter. Being very strict with themselves enabled them to carry on a seemingly normal life in the presence of the occasional bird, spider or mouse. The phobia was always there, though, lurking in the background, waiting to take over from reason and rational thought.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 31-36, choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 31. The writer was reading this magazine in the first place because
options: A. he was interested in its contents.
B. he had brought it with him.
C. he had chosen it deliberately.
D. he had time to kill.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Người viết cho biết ông đang ở trong phòng chờ của nha sĩ ('dentist's waiting room') với rất nhiều người xếp hàng phía trước. Ông nhặt ngẫu nhiên một tờ tạp chí trên bàn ('picked a magazine at random'). Điều này chứng tỏ ông đọc tạp chí chỉ để giết thời gian trong khi chờ đến lượt khám ('time to kill'). Bẫy: (A) sai vì ông không quan tâm từ đầu mà chỉ cuốn hút sau khi bắt đầu đọc, (B) sai vì tạp chí của phòng khám chứ không phải do ông tự mang, (C) sai vì ông nhặt ngẫu nhiên chứ không hề chọn lựa có chủ đích. Chuyên đề: Reading — Inference.
questions.answerLocation: Recently I found myself in the dentist's waiting room... I picked a magazine at random
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 32. The writer’s previous ideas about phobias
options: A. were the result of reading a survey.
B. revealed a limited understanding of the subject.
C. were based on how he had seen women behave.
D. came about after careful consideration.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tác giả thú nhận trước đây ông 'chưa từng nghĩ nhiều về điều đó' ('never thought too much about it') và tưởng rằng phobia chỉ chủ yếu xảy ra ở phụ nữ và xoay quanh nỗi sợ côn trùng hoặc chuột. Bài khảo sát này đã hoàn toàn làm thay đổi quan điểm của ông, chứng tỏ hiểu biết trước đây của ông cực kỳ hạn hẹp. Bẫy: (C) sai vì ông chỉ bị ảnh hưởng thụ động bởi các phim hoạt hình ('conditioned by cartoons') chứ không dựa trên việc trực tiếp quan sát hành vi của phụ nữ ngoài đời thực, (A) và (D) sai vì ông chưa từng suy nghĩ nghiêm túc trước khi đọc bài báo. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: I’d never thought too much about it... perceived these people as being mostly women... rodent or insects
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 33. The writer’s reaction to this article was
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Cụm từ 'My eyes were opened by this article' (Mắt tôi đã được khai sáng bởi bài viết này) thể hiện trạng thái vô cùng kinh ngạc và bất ngờ trước những phát hiện khảo sát mới mẻ về thế giới nỗi sợ phong phú mà trước đây ông không ngờ tới. Bẫy: (A) sai vì ông tiếp nhận thông tin một cách say sưa và nghiêm túc ('deeply engrossed', 'fascinated'), (B) sai vì khảo sát cho thấy những người tham gia sợ hãi bay ngượng ngùng chứ bản thân tác giả không ngượng ngùng, (D) sai vì ông không hề cảm thấy sợ hãi. Chuyên đề: Reading — Author's reaction.
questions.answerLocation: My eyes were opened by this article which was based on the results of a survey.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 34. According the writer,
options: A. a phobia is an irrational fear.
B. a fear is an irrational phobia.
C. a fear and a phobia are the same.
D. there is little similarity between a fear and a phobia.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tác giả phân tích rõ ràng định nghĩa ở đoạn 3: 'one of the main hallmarks of a phobia is that it is completely irrational. Strictly speaking, that is the difference between a fear and a phobia.' Nỗi sợ (fear) là phản ứng tự nhiên hợp lý trước mối nguy hiểm có thật (ví dụ rắn độc), trong khi hội chứng sợ (phobia) là nỗi hoảng loạn vô lý trước sinh vật vô hại. Chuyên đề: Reading — Vocabulary & Definitions.
questions.answerLocation: one of the main hallmarks of a phobia is that it is completely irrational. Strictly speaking, that is the difference
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 35. The phrase ‘This is the stuff of nightmares and horror films’ (lines 51-52) refers to
options: A. being in physical contact with birds or spiders.
B. strange people who are fond of snakes and spiders.
C. seeing birds’ claws or a spider’s legs.
D. seeing a spider walking across the floor.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Cụm từ 'This is the stuff of nightmares...' đứng ngay sau câu 'nearly everyone imagined the spider or the bird touching them' (hầu hết mọi người đều tưởng tượng ra cảnh con nhện hay con chim chạm vào da thịt họ). Như vậy, cụm từ ám chỉ nỗi kinh hoàng tột độ khi có sự tiếp xúc trực tiếp về mặt vật lý ('physical contact') với loài vật sợ hãi. Bẫy: (C) và (D) chỉ là các tác nhân thị giác khơi gợi trí tưởng tượng chứ bản thân chúng chưa phải là thứ trực tiếp được so sánh với 'stuff of nightmares'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Reference words.
questions.answerLocation: nearly everyone imagined the spider or the bird touching them. This is the stuff of nightmares
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 36. Which best sums up people’s attitudes to their phobias?
options: B. They manage to get over them.
C. They have to learn to live with them.
D. They think they are not important.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn cuối thảo luận cách mọi người sống chung với nỗi sợ: 'how they managed to live with their phobias... Being very strict with themselves enabled them to carry on a seemingly normal life... The phobia was always there, though'. Mọi người học cách kiểm soát lý trí và thích nghi để chung sống hòa bình với nỗi sợ hơn là chữa trị dứt điểm. Bẫy: (A) sai vì nhiều người vẫn nỗ lực đối mặt ('tried to tackle it'), (B) sai vì nỗi sợ vẫn luôn lẩn khuất phía sau chứ không biến mất hoàn toàn. Chuyên đề: Reading — Global synthesis.
questions.answerLocation: Being very strict with themselves enabled them to carry on a seemingly normal life... The phobia was always there
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about architecture and society. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Disposable Buildings?

Look at a building, any building. What can it tell you? Most people would agree that the architecture of a certain period reflects the taste and style of that period.

[ Gap 37 ]

Today’s architectural landmarks tend to be more secular than religious. For our present purpose, however, it is less important to know who commissioned a building than to look at it carefully. So, look around at today’s buildings: what do you see? You see odd shapes and garish colours that jar. Seeing one modern building does very little to prepare you for the next one; there is little or no uniformity.

[ Gap 38 ]

In the larger scheme of things, though, these differences are minor, and we can safely say that uniformity of appearance is one of the things that differentiates between the buildings of the past and those of the present. Another important difference, and one so obvious that it may seem to go without saying, is that modern buildings do not look like old buildings, (unless they are built in imitation, like neo-Classical architecture, for example).

[ Gap 39 ]

This is more than a comment on the quality of the respective building materials. The Pyramids were built to last; the Millennium Dome most assuredly was not. Of course this is not to say that the intention for modern structures is that they should last a certain amount of time and then fall down — a kind of disposable building. However, it is true to say that they seem to be designed and built with only the very immediate future in mind.

[ Gap 40 ]

The people of the past, on the other hand, looked ahead. It is clear that they wanted their buildings to be there for future generations. This is corroborated by the fact that, in countries where the climate allows it, they planted trees. Think about it: planting a tree, especially one that will some day grow to be very big, is the ultimate in altruistic behaviour. When you plant an oak sapling, you know very well that you will not see the tree that it will become.

[ Gap 41 ]

There is a third element in today’s way of thinking that is particularly relevant to architecture, and that is the aesthetic element. This is a difficult area to deal with because it is extremely subjective. Beauty is, indeed, very often in the eye of the beholder; we all know what we like, and we all like different things. Even allowing for this, however, most people would probably agree that ‘beautiful’ is not the word they would use to describe the majority of modern buildings.

[ Gap 42 ]

With most modern buildings, we certainly get one. I say this with total objectivity. I believe that it can be a very positive thing to be confronted with something completely different, even something that is a bit shocking. It provokes a reaction. It makes us think. All art changes as time goes by, and architecture in all its varied manifestations is a form of art.

[ Gap 43 ]

The result of all this has been that we have learnt a lot about cultures of past eras. What will be left behind after us? If we believe that the past has any value or anything to teach us, and if we believe that we bring the past with us into the future, this view has not been reflected in our architecture. The generations of the future will not benefit from us the way we have benefited from the generations of the past.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43).

questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn H bắt đầu với 'Buildings, however, tell us considerably more than that' (Tuy nhiên, các tòa nhà cho chúng ta biết nhiều hơn thế nữa). Từ 'that' trực tiếp phản hồi lại ý kiến ở đoạn trước: 'architecture of a certain period reflects the taste and style of that period'. Đoạn H cũng đề cập đến khái niệm 'rich and/or the powerful' đứng sau việc tài trợ thiết kế các công trình, kết nối tự nhiên với câu đầu tiên của đoạn kế sau: 'Today's architectural landmarks tend to be more secular than religious... less important to know who commissioned...' Chuyên đề: Coherence & Reference words.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 37 — between taste/style reflections and today's commissioned landmarks
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Đoạn F bắt đầu bằng cụm trạng từ tương phản 'Ancient buildings of the same era, on the other hand, resemble each other' (Mặt khác, các tòa nhà cổ thuộc cùng một thời kỳ lại giống nhau). Đây là phép đối lập hoàn hảo với câu cuối đoạn trước: 'Seeing one modern building does very little to prepare you for the next one; there is little or no uniformity' (kiến trúc hiện đại không có sự đồng nhất). Đoạn F lấy ví dụ về nhà thờ Gothic để minh họa tính đồng nhất này. Đoạn sau bắt đầu bằng 'In the larger scheme of things, though, these differences are minor', trong đó 'these differences' chính là 'myriad details and differences' ở cuối đoạn F. Chuyên đề: Contrast & Reference links.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 38 — between modern non-uniformity and ancient Gothic uniformity
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D bắt đầu bằng 'However, neither of these differences really tells us a lot about the people who built them...' (Tuy nhiên, cả hai sự khác biệt này đều không thực sự cho chúng ta biết nhiều...). 'Neither of these differences' (cả hai sự khác biệt này) ám chỉ hai sự khác biệt được thảo luận trước đó: sự đồng nhất ngoại hình và việc nhà hiện đại không giống nhà cổ. Cuối đoạn D cảnh báo kiến trúc ngày nay khó lòng trường tồn. Đoạn tiếp theo tiếp nối bằng 'This is more than a comment on the quality of the respective building materials. The Pyramids were built to last...' phản hồi về mặt cấu trúc độ bền vững. Chuyên đề: Syntactic cohesion.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 39 — between the discussion of differences and commentary on materials quality
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn C mở đầu bằng góc nhìn cá nhân của tác giả: 'Personally, I have yet to see a modern building that has worn well...' (Cá nhân tôi chưa từng thấy tòa nhà hiện đại nào chống chịu tốt với thời gian...). Nó tiếp tục phân tích sự ngắn hạn của kiến trúc hiện đại được đề cập ở cuối đoạn trước: 'seem to be designed and built with only the very immediate future in mind'. Đoạn C kết bằng ý chỉ cần phục vụ mục đích thế hệ hiện tại là đủ. Đoạn sau tạo ra phản đề hoàn hảo: 'The people of the past, on the other hand, looked ahead... for future generations'. Chuyên đề: Logical sequencing.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 40 — between modern immediate-future design and ancient long-term looking ahead
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G bắt đầu bằng 'In contrast to this, today's tree-planting activity is largely commercial...' (Trái ngược với điều này, hoạt động trồng cây ngày nay mang nặng tính thương mại...). Cụm từ 'In contrast to this' tạo sự đối lập trực tiếp với mô tả hành động trồng cây sồi vị tha, lâu dài không màng lợi ích bản thân ở cuối đoạn trước. Đoạn G cũng chuyển tiếp so sánh thái độ trồng cây ngắn hạn với thái độ xây dựng các tòa nhà tạm bợ ngày nay. Chuyên đề: Semantic opposition.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 41 — between ancient altruistic tree planting and modern commercial tree planting
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E đặt ra một chuỗi câu hỏi: 'Why is this? Is it because of the eye of the beholder? Or is it simply because we do not require our buildings to be beautiful any more?'. Đây là sự phát triển logic trực tiếp cho câu kết ở đoạn trước: 'most people would probably agree that 'beautiful' is not the word they would use to describe the majority of modern buildings'. Đoạn E kết bằng nhận định: 'Maybe we want a challenge'. Đoạn sau tiếp nối tự nhiên: 'With most modern buildings, we certainly get one' (có một thử thách). Chuyên đề: Discourse flow (Question-Answer).
questions.answerLocation: Gap 42 — between the statement on beauty and the resolution to embrace a challenge
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A mở đầu bằng khẳng định: 'The fact remains, though, that up to the present day, all art forms have been made to last' (Tuy nhiên, thực tế là mọi hình thức nghệ thuật đều được tạo ra để tồn tại lâu dài). Đoạn này củng cố luận điểm nghệ thuật ở cuối đoạn trước: 'architecture... is a form of art'. Đoạn A bàn về khát vọng trường tồn của người xưa qua kiến trúc. Đoạn sau kết luận: 'The result of all this has been that we have learnt a lot about cultures of past eras'. Chuyên đề: Structural alignment.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 43 — between architecture as a form of art and learning about past cultures
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: The fact remains, though, that up to the present day, all art forms have been made to last. Paintings and sculptures without number — as well as buildings — bear witness to this. The people of the past always wanted to build buildings that would pass the test of time. They must have wanted something of themselves to live on into the future, probably for all sorts of reasons, bad as well as good.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: For architecture, patronage has always been important. While this method of financing a work of art is as old as the idea of art itself, it gathered huge momentum during the Renaissance. During this period, wealthy and powerful families vied with each other in the creation of the beautiful and the different. It was a way of buying into the immortality of the artist and architect.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: Personally, I have yet to see a modern building that has worn well, that doesn’t have leaks or damp stains or bits of its outer structure falling off. It is hard to believe that any edifice built in the last fifty years or so is not like this or, if it isn’t, will not soon be. These days, we don’t seem to care about what will happen to our buildings: once they serve our purpose and that of our children, that seems to be enough for us.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: However, neither of these differences really tells us a lot about the people who built them, apart from what they wanted buildings to look like and the fact that they wanted their buildings to resemble each other. Now, take a look at some old buildings. The fact that you can see them at all, the fact that they are still standing, is the key to the past. We do not know how long today’s architectural heritage will last, but the chances are that it will not stand the test of time.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: Why is this? Is it because of the eye of the beholder? Or is it simply because we do not require our buildings to be beautiful any more? Perhaps beauty has become architecturally superfluous, or just plain old-fashioned. It could be that the idea of beauty is too sentimental and sugary for us. Maybe the modern psyche demands something more stimulating and less easy than beauty. Maybe we want a challenge.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: Ancient buildings of the same era, on the other hand, resemble each other. Take the example of the Gothic cathedral. To the non-specialist, one Gothic cathedral looks much like the next; if you've seen one, you've seen them all. This view is correct, in the sense that there is a uniformity of style in every Gothic cathedral ever built. It takes the enthusiast to spot and appreciate the myriad details and differences.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: In contrast to this, today's tree-planting activity is largely commercial, influenced strongly by the thought of immediate gain. Trees are planted that will grow quickly and can be cut down in a relatively short space of time. The analogy between tree planting and the construction of buildings is a good one. Our attitude in both these areas shows thinking that is essentially short-term and typical of today’s society; we want everything immediately and, where possible, we want it to generate money.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: Buildings, however, tell us considerably more than that. They give us a deep insight into the mind and culture of the people responsible for their construction. All buildings have a purpose of some sort or other, usually determined by the person or body responsible for commissioning them. Throughout the course of history, buildings have generally been constructed at the instigation of the rich and/or the powerful - the products of politics, religion or both.

sections.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about the transformation of public libraries in the digital age. For questions 44-53, choose from the sections (A-E). The sections may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
The Evolution of Public Libraries

Section A: The Civic Sanctuary

Historically viewed as quiet repositories of the written word, public libraries are undergoing a profound spatial and conceptual rebirth. In many urban centers, the traditional mandate of silent book preservation has been superseded by a dynamic new vision: the library as a vibrant community living room and social incubator. Modern libraries are intentionally designed to stimulate civic engagement, offering open-plan spaces for dialogue, community workshops, and multicultural events. Proponents argue that in an increasingly atomized society, where physical 'third places' are rapidly disappearing, the library remains a vital civic sanctuary that actively combats social isolation. By reimagining these structures as lively community hubs, municipal authorities are transforming the way citizens interact with their local environment.

Section B: Navigating the Digital Frontier

As information becomes increasingly digitized and decentralized, the physical collection of books is no longer the sole anchor of the library's identity. Instead, public libraries have eagerly embraced the digital frontier, integrating high-tech tools such as virtual reality (VR) headsets, 3D printers, and digital media makerspaces into their physical footprint. In these high-tech labs, patrons can collaborate on digital design projects, record podcasts, or learn coding. This shift has radically altered the role of the librarian. No longer merely quiet gatekeepers of the stacks, modern librarians have transitioned into essential digital navigators, guiding users through the vast, complex, and often overwhelming landscape of online information, ensuring that technological literacy is democratically distributed.

Section C: Structural Monumentality and Adaptability

From an architectural perspective, the construction of a new public library is a profound opportunity to make a monumental statement about a city's civic values. Today's landmark libraries are architectural marvels, utilizing sustainable materials, natural light, and bold structural geometry to inspire awe and civic pride. However, these spaces must also prioritize structural adaptability. Unlike the rigid book stacks of the past, modern library interiors are characterized by modular furniture, movable walls, and flexible technological infrastructure. This ensures that the building can easily adapt to changing societal needs and technological innovations over the coming decades, preventing architectural obsolescence and ensuring the structure remains a functional asset for future generations.

Section D: The Sanctity of Contemplation

While the modernization of public libraries is widely celebrated, some scholars and bibliophiles urge caution, warning of the potential erosion of the library's core traditional value: the sanctity of quiet contemplation. In the rush to create buzzy, collaborative spaces and high-tech centers, there is a risk that the library's role as a quiet refuge for deep reading and intellectual solitude will be lost. Furthermore, critics argue that digital screens, however convenient, cannot fully replicate the tactile and sensory experience of physical media — the weight of a book in one's hand, the distinct scent of aged paper, and the psychological focus that physical print commands. They contend that in our hyper-connected, easily distracted age, the preservation of quiet, offline spaces is more revolutionary than any digital upgrade.

Section E: Bridging the Divide

Perhaps the most critical function of the contemporary public library is its role in bridging the digital divide for marginalized and disadvantaged groups. For many low-income families, homeless individuals, and recent immigrants, the library is a unique lifeline, offering free access to high-speed internet, computers, job application databases, and language learning software. Without these essential resources, these groups would be severely disadvantaged in a society that assumes universal digital access. However, directors warn that this democratic mission is increasingly threatened by commercial pressures. The rising cost of digital publisher licensing fees and complex paywalls restricts the library's ability to offer a comprehensive selection of e-books and databases, highlighting the ongoing struggle to keep knowledge truly open and free for all.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 44-53, choose from the sections A-E. The sections may be chosen more than once.

questions.instruction: 44. a warning about the potential erosion of a library's core traditional value
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D nêu rõ mối lo ngại: 'warning of the potential erosion of the library's core traditional value: the sanctity of quiet contemplation' (cảnh báo về sự xói mòn tiềm tàng đối với giá trị truyền thống cốt lõi: sự tôn nghiêm của việc chiêm nghiệm tĩnh lặng). Người viết lo ngại sự ồn ào hợp tác kỹ thuật số sẽ làm lu mờ đi không gian yên bình để đọc sâu. Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Textual matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: warning of the potential erosion of the library's core traditional value
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 45. how a library can act as a catalyst for local civic engagement
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Section A khẳng định các thư viện hiện đại đóng vai trò thúc đẩy sự gắn kết: 'Modern libraries are intentionally designed to stimulate civic engagement, offering open-plan spaces for dialogue, community workshops...' (được thiết kế có ý đồ nhằm kích thích gắn kết công dân, cung cấp không gian mở cho thảo luận, hội thảo...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Paraphrasing.
questions.answerLocation: Section A: Modern libraries are intentionally designed to stimulate civic engagement
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 46. the integration of cutting-edge technology to foster collaborative creativity
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B mô tả việc tích hợp thiết bị tối tân để thúc đẩy sáng tạo nhóm: 'integrating high-tech tools such as virtual reality (VR) headsets, 3D printers, and digital media makerspaces... patrons can collaborate on digital design projects, record podcasts...' Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Synthesis.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: integrating high-tech tools... patrons can collaborate on digital design projects
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 47. the role of libraries in providing essential resources to disadvantaged groups
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E tập trung vào vai trò thu hẹp khoảng cách xã hội: 'bridging the digital divide for marginalized and disadvantaged groups... lifeline, offering free access to high-speed internet, computers, job application databases...' (bắc cầu nối khoảng cách số cho các nhóm yếu thế... dòng sinh mệnh, cung cấp internet, máy tính miễn phí...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Group matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: bridging the digital divide for marginalized and disadvantaged groups
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 48. a description of library architecture as a physical expression of societal values
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C bàn luận về kiến trúc như một biểu tượng giá trị vật lý: 'make a monumental statement about a city's civic values. Today's landmark libraries are architectural marvels... to inspire awe and civic pride.' (kiến trúc như một tuyên bố hoành tráng biểu đạt các giá trị công dân thành phố). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Abstract concept matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: opportunity to make a monumental statement about a city's civic values.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 49. the shift in the primary role of librarians from gatekeepers to navigators
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B mô tả sự thay đổi của người thủ thư: 'No longer merely quiet gatekeepers of the stacks, modern librarians have transitioned into essential digital navigators...' (Không còn đơn thuần là người gác kho sách lặng lẽ, các thủ thư hiện đại đã trở thành người điều hướng số...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Role shifts.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: No longer merely quiet gatekeepers... modern librarians have transitioned into essential digital navigators
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 50. an acknowledgment that digital versions cannot fully replicate the sensory experience of physical media
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D lập luận về trải nghiệm giác quan: 'digital screens... cannot fully replicate the tactile and sensory experience of physical media — the weight of a book in one's hand, the distinct scent of aged paper...' (màn hình số không thể thay thế trải nghiệm xúc giác, xúc cảm của trang sách, cân nặng, mùi hương giấy cũ...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Sensory words.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: cannot fully replicate the tactile and sensory experience of physical media
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 51. the argument that library design should prioritize adaptability to future needs
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C nhấn mạnh khả năng thay đổi công năng: 'these spaces must also prioritize structural adaptability... interiors are characterized by modular furniture... to easily adapt to changing societal needs...' (ưu tiên khả năng thích ứng cấu trúc... dễ dàng biến đổi theo sự thay đổi công nghệ xã hội tương lai). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Future-proof design.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: spaces must also prioritize structural adaptability... prevent architectural obsolescence
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 52. the idea that modern libraries can help combat social isolation
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Section A chỉ ra tác dụng kết nối xã hội: 'where physical 'third places' are rapidly disappearing, the library remains a vital civic sanctuary that actively combats social isolation.' (thư viện như một thánh đường xã hội giúp chủ động chống lại sự cô độc, cô lập xã hội). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Social dynamics.
questions.answerLocation: Section A: the library remains a vital civic sanctuary that actively combats social isolation.
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 53. a concern that the commercialization of digital resources could restrict access
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E nêu lo ngại về rào cản thương mại: 'mission is increasingly threatened by commercial pressures. The rising cost of digital publisher licensing fees and complex paywalls restricts the library's ability to offer...' (sứ mệnh dân chủ bị đe dọa bởi phí bản quyền tăng cao và paywall làm giới hạn khả năng phục vụ miễn phí). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Financial barriers.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: democratic mission is increasingly threatened by commercial pressures... licensing fees and complex paywalls
questions.source: Practice Test 2, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3
